title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
An old man named Quetcham recounts past conflicts to Vane. He describes an argument three days prior where Mr. Bread crossed the bay to confront Striker in the woods. Roman, described as a man of great strength, intervened and forcibly removed Mr. Bread from the island, prompting Bread to vow to bankrupt his opponents to settle the score. Quetcham also details a fight involving Jonah Lincoln, who lives at Bread's place. Lincoln's sister stripped naked in front of Roman to protest her brother's control; when Lincoln slapped Roman in response, Roman threw Lincoln into the bay.

Following this, Vane, Professor Adley, and an agitated Dr. Temple return to the mainland via police boat. While Ellery and Professor Yardley spend a quiet evening discussing university memories, Dr. Temple departs his home in the middle of the night. He moves stealthily through the woods of Bradwood and toward the Lein residence. 

Crouching near a window at the Lein house, Dr. Temple eavesdrops on a tense conversation between Percy and Elizabeth Lein. They discuss something that has been buried and mention a "Bundy Reine incident" in post-war Budapest. Elizabeth expresses fear, while Percy insists they will be safe. After the couple falls silent, Dr. Temple retreats through the woods toward the pier. There, he observes a man and a woman in a rowboat near the shore. The woman pleads to be taken to the island for safety, but the man warns her that the situation is dangerous and that they should remain split up until the crisis passes.
